attributes.action: Make general anesthetic a part of the standard procedure for IUD and IUS coil
attributes.additional_details: After doing some research and asking many other women online it came to my attention that there are many horrific accounts of women's pain not being taken seriously when it comes to medical procedures. They have not been offered pain relief or told to just put up with it and it has resulted in many people being in serious pain after this invasive procedure.
attributes.background: I would like the UK government to make general anesthetic part of the standard procedure when inserting the IUD (copper coil) and IUS (marina coil)

departments.name: Department of Health and Social Care

rejection.details:
Your petition is about something that the Scottish Government and Scottish Parliament is responsible for. That means that the UK Government and Parliament can't look into it. 

The Scottish Parliament has its own Public Petitions Committee. If you'd like to send your petition to the Scottish Parliament, you can find out more here: https://petitions.parliament.scot/

You may also wish to contact your local council and MSP who represent you about this issue. 

You can find the name of your local council and how to contact them here: https://www.mygov.scot/find-your-local-council

You can find the name of your local MSP and how to contact them here: https://www.parliament.scot/msps
